affordances.name: Binding constraint throughput check
affordances.mechanism: When a plan spreads effort across many pain points, require the answer to identify the verified throughput limiter, the queue or dependency it controls, and the single choke point that would still throttle the system if other work were funded.

use_when: Local optimization is masking the true throughput limiter.
Sequence and capacity matter more than aggregate effort.
Fixing one choke point could unlock disproportionate downstream gain.
A migration, launch, or handoff plan assumes every workstream can move in parallel.
do_not_use_when: The team labels the noisiest queue, most visible complaint, or highest-status pain point as the bottleneck without verifying actual system throughput.
A roadmap treats all workstreams as equally movable even though one approval, integration, or staffing dependency is pacing everything else.
The bottleneck label is being used to avoid the harder question of how the fix will actually be executed.
case_evidence_needed: The system, process, launch, migration, or handoff under review.
The visible queues, dependencies, or capacity constraints.
Evidence that one step is actually limiting system throughput.
The downstream gain expected if that choke point is relieved.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to distinguish the visible pain point from the verified binding constraint and name what evidence would prove the choke point is pacing the whole system.
evidence_required: The system output or throughput measure.
The queue, approval, integration, staffing, or specialist dependency.
The effect expected if that constraint is relieved before other improvements.
good_output_shape: Explains why other busy areas are not the binding constraint.
States the first measure that should move when the bottleneck is relieved.

treatment_requirements.description: After identifying the bottleneck, require the answer to state how the constrained step will actually be relieved rather than stopping at the label.
evidence_required: The specific constrained step or dependency.
The execution action that would increase capacity, reduce queueing, change sequencing, or remove the gating dependency.
The owner, prerequisite, or implementation constraint that determines whether the relief action can happen.
good_output_shape: Names how the constrained step will improve.
Separates bottleneck diagnosis from bottleneck relief.
Identifies the owner or prerequisite required to execute the relief action.

diagnostic_questions: Which step would still throttle the whole system if every other improvement were funded?
What evidence shows this is the binding constraint rather than the loudest complaint?
Which dependency, queue, approval, or specialist function is pacing everything else?
How will the constrained step actually improve once we name it?
misuse_guards: Do not treat the noisiest queue or highest-status pain point as the bottleneck without throughput evidence.
Do not parallelize around one hidden gating dependency.
Do not identify the what of a constraint while ignoring the how of relieving it.

source_evidence.source_quote: local optimization is masking the true throughput limiter
sequence and capacity matter more than aggregate effort
Which step would still throttle the whole system if I funded every other improvement on the list?
overlooking the difficulty of figuring out *how* to execute the solution

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports one binding-constraint check centered on throughput, sequence, capacity, gating dependencies, and PR78's execution path for actually relieving the constrained step.
dropped_material: Did not extract cognitive load, prompt context windows, or communication focus as separate affordances.
Kept queue, migration, launch, and handoff examples under one throughput check.
open_questions: PR40 should test whether this card makes execution packets more concrete about what must be unblocked first.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that the noisiest queue, most visible complaint, or highest-status pain point is not necessarily the bottleneck unless actual throughput verifies it.

source_evidence.source_quote: teams label the noisiest queue, most visible complaint, or highest-status pain point as the bottleneck without verifying actual system throughput

absence_records.reason: The source rejects roadmap logic that treats workstreams as equally movable when one approval, integration, or staffing dependency is pacing everything else.

source_evidence.source_quote: a roadmap treats all workstreams as equally movable even though one approval, integration, or staffing dependency is pacing everything else
every system, regardless of its complexity, has a point of greatest restriction

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Bottlenecks. Extracted one binding constraint check, rejected loudest-pain and parallelization-over-gating-dependency errors, and PR78 hardens the treatment to require an execution path for relieving the constrained step.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later execution-packet review should test whether this card helps identify the real pacing dependency rather than an attractive pain point.
